risk_if_not_implemented: Without Emergency Accounts, unauthorized users may gain access to systems or data, increasing risk of data breaches and insider threats.

compensating_control_1.name: Periodic Review of Account Privileges
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to periodically-review the privileges assigned to individuals and service accounts to validate the need for such privileges and reassign or remove unnecessary privileges, as necessary.
compensating_control_1.justification: Periodic Review of Account Privileges (IAC-17) provides periodic assessment and assurance that compensates for the absence of Emergency Accounts (IAC-15.9) by providing structured evaluation of the environment to surface gaps and verify residual control effectiveness. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: User Provisioning & De-Provisioning
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to utilize a formal user registration and de-registration process that governs the assignment of access rights.
compensating_control_2.justification: User Provisioning & De-Provisioning (IAC-07) provides access control enforcement that compensates for the absence of Emergency Accounts (IAC-15.9) by restricting system and data access to authorized users through alternative identity and access mechanisms. Given the technology-focused nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
